title: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and ConvertKit: pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your needs best.
h1: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit: The Complete Comparison
intro: Mailchimp and ConvertKit are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Mailchimp targets small businesses and beginners with affordable pricing and broad functionality, while ConvertKit focuses on creators, bloggers, and course sellers with specialized features. This comparison will help you determine which platform is the right fit for your email marketing needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: Drag-drop editor, A/B testing, landing pages
comparison_table.tool_b: Commerce features, visual automations, creator-focused design

choose_a_if: You're a beginner or small business with a limited budget
You need affordable A/B testing and landing page builders
You want the most generous free tier to test features
choose_b_if: You're a creator, blogger, or online course seller
You need built-in commerce and product sales features
You prefer a platform purpose-built for creator economics

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your use case. Mailchimp excels for beginners and small businesses due to lower pricing and accessibility, while ConvertKit is superior for creators and course sellers thanks to its specialized features and commerce capabilities. ConvertKit has better customer reviews on Trustpilot (3.2 vs 1.5), suggesting stronger user satisfaction overall.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or ConvertKit?
faq.a: Mailchimp is significantly cheaper, starting at $13/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Mailchimp's free tier also accommodates 500 subscribers versus ConvertKit's 1,000, though both offer solid freemium options for getting started.

faq.q: Can I switch from ConvertKit to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and migrate to the other service. Most email marketing platforms support CSV exports, making the transition straightforward. However, you'll need to rebuild automations and sequences manually, as they don't transfer between platforms.
